free_text: Multiparameter study of 1H, 3H and 4He in fast
neutron induced fission of 235U
Thickness 5 mg/cm2, active area
4 cm2
Delta E (thickness 64 um) -
E(thickness 600 um) Semiconductor detector telescope.
The sample of 235U was used as the cathode of
the ionization chamber. A 7 mg/cm2 thick aluminium
foil placed at a distance of 2 mm was used as the
collector of the ionization chamber and also to stop
the natural alpha particles and the fission
fragments. The chamber was filled with high purity
argon gas to a pressure of 1 atm.
1H, 3H and 4He were detected in coincidence
with fission fragments.
To get keV neutrons
5 cm thick paraffin block was used to get
thermal neutrons.
Proton, Triton, Alpha particle and Fission
fragments were detected.
Particle identification from Delta E- E detector was
done by defining a parameter PI=(E+ Delta-E)**1.76-
E**1.76. This gave three distinct peaks for proton,
triton and alpha particles. The peaks corresponding
to protons, tritons and alpha-particles were
identified by carrying out a Monte Carlo calculation
for the geometrical configuration of the source
detector assembly and using the known energy spectra
of these particles in the thermal neutron fission.
No information is given.
By Compiler, the neutron energies mentioned in the
text are 120, 180, 230 and 550 keV. However, the
neutron energies given in the figures are 150, 217,
230 and 550 keV. Digitizaion results are close to 150,
217, 230 and 550 keV. Therefore the neutron energies
given in the figures are adopted.
Compiled by G. Mohanto
